info.name: Wondrous Items
info.description: Wondrous Items from Snorting Ogre Homebrew
info.author: jdukosse

description.p: When the wearer deals Thunder, Lightning, or Radiant damage, the target takes an extra 1d4 Thunder damage and must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or fall prone.

Legends say a Giant Elk and a druid once formed a powerful friendship. When the Elk laid down his life for the druid, she crafted this helmet in honour of her beloved friend, so his beauty and compassion would live on forever.

ul.li: High Spellcasting: You gain a +1 bonus to Spell Save DC.
Seldom Caught Unawares: You gain a +1 bonus to Initiative rolls.

element.name: Boots of the Flickering Footpad
element.description:
These dark leather boots leave faint afterimages with every step. When their magic is invoked, the wearer vanishes in a swirl of grey smoke, leaving behind a fleeting smoky silhouette.\
Flicker Step: As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. This functions as the Misty Step spell. You can use this property twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Distracting Afterimage: When you use Flicker Step, a smoky duplicate remains in the space you left behind until the start of your next turn. The first creature that targets you with an attack before then must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or target the afterimage instead, causing the attack to automatically miss.
Fade into the Shadows: Immediately after using Flicker Step, you may take the Hide action as part of the same bonus action. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Shadow Footed: While you are in dim light or darkness, your walking speed increases by 10 feet.

element.name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Spirit Bear

description.p: A figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.
The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

Spirit Bear (Uncommon). This obsidian-black bear figurine is inlaid with silver lines that pulsate slightly. It can become a bear for up to 1 hour. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until after a short rest. While in bear form, the figurine attacks the nearest enemy unless you use your bonus action to make it do something else.

element.name: Wraps of Freedom
element.description:
These white-blue wraps have silver runes embroidered on them. They look a little worn and torn from constant wind.\
Memory of Escape: Advantage on checks and saves against being restrained, grappled, imprisoned, or magically compelled.
Break the Bond: Once per short rest, when you fail a saving throw against charm, fear, or domination, you can choose to succeed instead.
No Cage Shall Hold Me: As a reaction, spend 1 Ki point to automatically end one effect causing: Grappled Restrained Paralyzed (if magical, gain another save instead).
